**Becoming eager for belonging: enhanced interbrain synchrony during emotional interaction in social avoidance individuals after social rejection.** — Cerebral cortex (New York, N.Y. : 1991) (2025). PMID: 41364661. DOI: 10.1093/cercor/bhaf233. Score: 69. Socially avoidant individuals, who are vulnerable to interpersonal conflict and social rejection, are characterized as low social approach motivation and high social avoidance motivation. However, the neural underpinnings of the influence of social rejection on socially avoidant individuals have not been fully studied. This study utilized EEG-based hyperscanning to examine the interbrain synchrony (IBS) of 18 socially avoidant dyads and 18 comparison dyads during acooperative task. To examine t
